semantic.role: Infrastructure layer defining the Runtime I/O contract (interface) and a default console-based implementation for logging and user confirmation.
responsibilities: Define the `RuntimeIO` interface as a stable abstraction for runtime output (info, warn, error) and user confirmation (confirm).
Provide `defaultRuntimeIO`, a concrete implementation using `console.log`, `console.warn`, `console.error` for output and delegating confirm to `promptForExplicitConfirmation`.
Serve as a public infra facade that other modules can depend on for I/O without coupling directly to console or prompt implementations.
outOfScope: File system or network I/O operations.
Any non-console output mechanisms (e.g., GUI dialogs, remote logging).
Buffering or formatting of log messages beyond simple console use.
invariants: The `RuntimeIO` interface must remain backwards-compatible to avoid breaking consumers.
The default implementation must not introduce side effects beyond console I/O and prompt for confirmation.
Callers should prefer importing `defaultRuntimeIO` from this module rather than reaching into deeper implementation paths (per infra-facade-imports rule).

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Establish a stable, swappable I/O boundary for runtime interactions so that CLI, MCP server, and other components can operate without direct console dependencies.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: No specific recent change; the file is part of the v1.0.0 release baseline.

publicSurface.description: Interface defining the contract for runtime output (info, warn, error) and user confirmation. Any consumer that requires logging or prompts should depend on this interface.
Default implementation of RuntimeIO that logs to console and prompts using promptForExplicitConfirmation. Used as the standard runtime I/O object across the codebase.
